frozen_components: encoder weights (frozen after initial training, used only at inference)
architecture.summary: A neurosymbolic method that intercepts LLM hidden states at layer 17, encodes them into fixed-dimensional HRR (Holographic Reduced Representation) vectors via a trained linear encoder, applies rule-based symbolic algorithms (modulo, GCD, LCM, bitwise ops, etc.) within the VSA space by querying digit and operation components, decodes the solution vector back to hidden-state space via a trained linear decoder, and merges the decoded result (50-50 weighted sum) with the original hidden state before passing to downstream layers. Inference gates the intervention via dot-product similarity threshold (0.8) to skip unknown problem types.
trained_components: linear encoder network (hidden state → VSA neurosymbolic vector)
linear decoder network (VSA neurosymbolic vector → hidden state)
decoder fine-tuning by cross-entropy loss on output logits

biblio.title: Improving Rule-based Reasoning in LLMs using Neurosymbolic Representations
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the 2025 Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing (EMNLP), pages 30577--30596

claims.dataset: Symbolic-Math Dataset (10 operation types: modulo, multiplication, GCD, LCM, square modulo, bitwise AND/XOR/OR, addition, integer division)
claims.metric: cross-entropy loss reduction and accuracy improvement factor
claims.statement: average of 88.6% lower cross-entropy loss and 15.4 times more problems correctly solved on a suite of mathematical reasoning tasks compared to chain-of-thought prompting and supervised fine-tuning (LoRA)
claims.unit: percent reduction in loss; 15.4x accuracy improvement

claims.dataset: Symbolic-Math Dataset, modulo operation type, 2000 test examples
claims.statement: On modulo tasks, NS LLM achieves 98.7% accuracy with 0.093 loss, compared to Standard LLM 53.5% / 2.904 loss, CoT 69.7% / 4.424 loss, LoRA 51.5% / 3.838 loss

claims.dataset: Symbolic-Math Dataset, multiplication operation type, 2000 test examples
claims.statement: On multiplication tasks, NS LLM achieves 95.6% accuracy versus Standard LLM 1.1%, CoT 25.3%, LoRA 4.5%

claims.metric: RMSE loss and classification error rate
claims.statement: Encoder achieves minimum RMSE loss at layer 17 of LLaMA 3.1 8B; encoding error rate for individual digits is under 2% at this layer
claims.unit: percent error; lower bound on RMSE loss (not numerical)

claims.dataset: Symbolic-Math Dataset, out-of-distribution operation types (Addition, Integer Division)
claims.statement: Out-of-distribution tasks (Addition and Integer Division, unseen during training) show NS LLM achieving 98.2% and 97.4% accuracy respectively, compared to LoRA's 46.5% and 72.0%, demonstrating generalization by gating interventions on problem-type similarity

Computational overhead of neurosymbolic processing per forward pass is O(D²) in VSA dimension D, dominated by standard KV-cached transformer cost of O(n²) (sequence length n), and space overhead is O(D²), negligible relative to full LLM memory

claims.dataset: Symbolic-Math Dataset, LCM and Square Modulo operation types
claims.statement: On more complex tasks such as LCM (87.3% score) and square modulo (58.9% score), NS LLM performance is lower, likely due to encoding error accumulation and the complexity of forward-pass algorithms requiring multi-hop reasoning that a single decoder intervention cannot fully capture

CoT baseline exhibits higher loss (0.874–16.606 across tasks) and inconsistent improvements; on bitwise XOR CoT degrades from 6.7% (Standard) to 1.1%, attributed to error accumulation in multi-step intermediate reasoning steps such as incorrect bitstring conversion
